# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Jenkins Pipeline that builds Docker images with user-provided image tags and registry URLs. Accept these as build parameters and use them directly in docker build and push commands. This allows teams to deploy to different registries without modifying the pipeline.
# Category: cicd_security

pipeline {
    agent any

    parameters {
        string(name: 'REGISTRY_URL', defaultValue: 'docker.io/myusername', description: 'Full Docker Registry path for the image (e.g., myregistry.com/myteam)')
        string(name: 'REGISTRY_CREDENTIALS_ID', defaultValue: 'docker-credentials', description: 'Jenkins Secret Text Credential ID for Docker Registry login (e.g., docker-hub-creds)')
        string(name: 'APP_NAME', defaultValue: 'my-app', description: 'Name of the application Docker image')
        string(name: 'IMAGE_TAG', defaultValue: 'latest', description: 'Tag for the Docker image (e.g., 1.0.0, develop)')
    }

    stages {
        stage('Checkout') {
            steps {
                // Replace with your SCM details (e.g., Git)
                git branch: 'main', url: 'https://github.com/your-org/your-repo.git'
            }
        }

        stage('Build and Push Docker Image') {
            steps {
                script {
                    def fullImageName = "${params.REGISTRY_URL}/${params.APP_NAME}:${params.IMAGE_TAG}"
                    def registryHost = params.REGISTRY_URL.split('/')[0] // Extracts "docker.io" or "myregistry.com"

                    echo "Building Docker image: ${fullImageName}"
                    sh "docker build -t ${fullImageName} ."

                    echo "Pushing Docker image to registry: ${registryHost}"
                    withDockerRegistry(credentialsId: params.REGISTRY_CREDENTIALS_ID, url: "https://${registryHost}") {
                        sh "docker push ${fullImageName}"
                    }
                }
            }
        }
    }
}
